contents.title: Start Here
contents.description: Create, Edit and Share Meaningful Maps

children.value: Create, Edit and Share Meaningful Maps

Waymark JS is a JavaScript library for creating and sharing geographical information. It is designed to be easy to use and intuitive, and is suitable for a wide range of applications due to its flexibility and customisation
as the default Basemap. Waymark JS stores data in GeoJSON format, with support for GPX and KML files.
Display a Map with a custom Marker.
// The Title is used to create the "pub" Type Key
"Great pub, great food! Especially after a Long Ride 🚴🍔🍟🍺🍺💤"
To use Waymark JS, you will need to include the following assets in your page. Here we are adding them to the
of the document so they are immediately available to the

props.code:
<!-- jQuery (required) -->
<script src="https://code.jquery.com/jquery-3.7.1.min.js"></script>

<!-- Waymark CSS & JavaScript -->
<link rel="stylesheet" href="dist/css/waymark-js.min.css" />
<script src="dist/js/waymark-js.min.js"></script>

children.value: tags could be placed at the end of the
global to be available before creating a Map. If you are not already using
, you can include it from a CDN as shown above.
To display a Map, place an empty
in the body of your document. If you plan to display just one Map on the page, you can use the default
To initialise the Map, we need to add a
tag to the document. This should be placed
the Map Container and JS/CSS assets have been included.
Here we are creating a new Viewer Instance, setting some
, and adding a Marker to the Map.
For multiple Maps, provide the unique

props.code:
<!-- Map Initialisation -->
<script>
  // Create a Viewer Instance
  const viewer = window.Waymark_Map_Factory.viewer();

  // Initialise with our options
  viewer.init({
    map_options: {
      // Initial Map Zoom
      map_init_zoom: 16,

      // Define a new Type
      marker_types: [
        {
          // The title is used to create the "pub" Type Key
          marker_title: "Pub",
          marker_icon: "ion-beer",
          marker_colour: "brown",
        },
      ],
    },
  });

  // Load a Marker from GeoJSON
  viewer.load_json({
    type: "FeatureCollection",
    features: [
      {
        type: "Feature",
        properties: {
          // The "pub" Type Key
          type: "pub",
          title: "The Scarlet Ibis",
          description:
            "Great pub, great food! Especially after a Long Ride 🚴🍔🍟🍺🍺💤",
          image_large_url: "https://www.waymark.dev/assets/geo/pub.jpeg",
        },
        geometry: {
          type: "Point",
          coordinates: [-128.0094, 50.6539],
        },
      },
    ],
  });
</script>

children.value: // The title is used to create the "pub" Type Key
"Great pub, great food! Especially after a Long Ride 🚴🍔🍟🍺🍺💤"

Each Type has a unique Key that is used to identify it (e.g.

property, so Type Titles should be unique.

contents.title: Map
contents.description: The Map is the central component of Waymark JS. It is used to display Basemaps, Overlays and an interface for interacting with the Map.

children.value: The Map is the central component of Waymark JS. It is used to display Basemaps, Overlays (Markers, Lines and Shapes) as well as an interface for interacting with the Map. The Map can be used in two modes: Viewer and Editor.
Maps can either be read-only using the Viewer, or editable using the Editor. Both the Viewer and Editor extend the
Multiple maps can be used on a single page, each with their own configuration. A single Map is known as an Instance and is created using the
object. To create a new Instance, use the

props.code:
// Create a Viewer Instance
const viewer = window.Waymark_Map_Factory.viewer();

children.value: Once an Instance has been created, it must be initialised with a configuration in order to display a Map.
Each Instance is provided its own configuration, as a JavaScript object, which is passed to the
method, which accepts a GeoJSON object as an argument.
The object is expected to be a
, even if only one Feature is being added.
The current state of the Map is accessible through the
object, which contains the current GeoJSON data. This is a Leaflet
and provides the current state of the Map in GeoJSON using the

props.code:
// Get GeoJSON data
const map_data = viewer.map_data.toGeoJSON();

children.value: The Viewer can be used to display
with a single Marker, or many Overlays (Markers, Lines and Shapes). Each Overlay can be given a Title, Description and Image URL, which are displayed in a Popup when the Overlay is clicked.
To create a Viewer Instance, use the

props.code:
// Create a Viewer Instance
const viewer = window.Waymark_Map_Factory.viewer();

children.value: Overlays can be categorised using Types, provided to the
. These allow you to define custom Icons, Colours and more. To associate an Overlay to a Type, set the
property in the GeoJSON feature to the relevant
The Viewer has some features that are not available in the Editor, such as the Image Gallery, Overlay Filter and Elevation Profile.

The Editor can be used to create, edit and delete Overlays. It has a similar interface to the Viewer, but with additional controls for adding and editing Overlays. The Editor can also be used to import and export GeoJSON data, which can be used to save and load Maps.
To Create an Editor Instance, use the

props.code:
// Create an Editor Instance
const editor = window.Waymark_Map_Factory.editor();

method, which accepts a GeoJSON object. Every time the Map is edited, the Map is converted to GeoJSON and output into (the inner HTML of) a

Map options are available to both the Viewer and Editor.

The Name will appear in a dropdown list shown by the Map when multiple Basemaps have been entered.
Many mapping services support the Slippy Map format. Waymark requires URLs that contain

This is an example of a Basemap configuration:
OpenStreetMap is the default Basemap. If you do not provide any Basemaps, OpenStreetMap will be used.

Customise how Overlays are displayed on the Map. When you add an Overlay (Marker, Line or Shape) to the Map you may want to style it in a certain way. In the case of Markers, you may want to use certain icons and colours.
Types allow you set these styles once, using the
arrays. Once a type has been provided, an Overlay can be assigned to it using the

array, each Marker Type is an object with the following options:

Which shape of Marker to use. Circles and Squares are centered at the specified location, Markers point down to that location.

Font Icons are available from Font Awesome and Ionic Icons. Simple Text or Emojis are supported, as well as custom HTML.

Type Keys are a unique string that identifies a Type. The Type Title is used to create the Type Key, by removing any non-alpha-numeric characters and converting it to lowercase.
The Type Key for "Pub" would be
. The Type Key for "A Much Longer Title" would be
When assigning a Type to an Overlay, the
feature property must match the Type Key. For example, to assign the "Pub" Type to a Marker:
Waymark JS adds the Type Key to the Overlay using the
class. For example, the "Pub" Type would be:

contents.title: Viewer
contents.description: The Viewer can be used to display Basemaps with a single Marker, or many Overlays (Markers, Lines and Shapes).

children.value: The Viewer can be used to display
with a single Marker, or many Overlays (Markers, Lines and Shapes). Each Overlay can be given a Title, Description and Image URL, which are displayed in a Popup when the Overlay is clicked.
– Thumbnails for Markers currently in view on the Map are displayed and can be clicked open the Marker popup.
– Pick which Overlays are currently visible by
– Markers that are close together are stacked, to declutter the Map.
– An interactive elevation profile for Lines that have elevation data.
To create a Viewer Instance, use the

props.code:
// Create a Viewer Instance
const viewer = window.Waymark_Map_Factory.viewer();

children.value: Options are passed to the Instance
object, are used to customise the Viewer Mode.

Whether to display an image gallery for Markers that have images. Thumbnails for Markers currently in view on the Map are displayed and clicking on a thumbnail will centre the Map on the Marker and open the popup.
Allow the user to filter which Overlays are currently visible on Map.
Whether to cluster (stack) Markers that are close together, to declutter the Map.
The maximum radius that a cluster will cover from the central Marker (in pixels). Decreasing will make more, smaller clusters. Default
Markers will not be clustered above this zoom level.
Whether to display an interactive elevation profile graph below the Map for Lines that have elevation data.
The ID of the HTML element to contain the elevation profile graph. The default is
and if this element does not exist, it will be created
Display elevation data in metric (m/km) or imperial (ft/mi) units.
The colour of the elevation graph and associated Line, provided as a CSS colour (e.g.
Whether to show the elevation profile initially when the Map loads, or wait for a Line to be clicked.
How many seconds before scroll zoom is enabled.
seconds will mean no delay (disabling this feature). A large number of seconds like
, meaning the user will need to
Whether to display a message while scroll zoom is disabled.
The message to display while scroll zoom is disabled.
This configuration enables all available Viewer features:
Once the configuration is set, the
method is called to initialise the Map:

props.code:
// Initialise with our options
viewer.init(config);

children.value: The Map is now displayed on the page according to the provided options.
Waymark JS supports loading data from
After the Map is initialised, data can be loaded using the
To load a GeoJSON file using
promise to ensure the data is available:
To load data from a GPX or KML file, the file is fetched and parsed as XML, then converted to GeoJSON using the included
methods. The resulting GeoJSON is then added to the Map.

contents.title: Editor
contents.description: The Editor Mode allows users to create and edit Overlays on a Map.

children.value: The Editor Mode allows users to create and edit Overlays (Markers, Lines, and Shapes) on a Map. Each Overlay can be customised with a Title, Description, Image URL and categorised using
Every time the Map is edited, the Map is converted to GeoJSON and output into (the inner HTML of) a
element. This can be used to easily integrate Waymark JS with a form. You can specify the container ID using the
will be created automatically if the container is not found.
Waymark JS does not currently handle reading from file. You can see how Waymark JS can be integrated with a WordPress back-end to handle this

props.code:
// Create an Editor Instance
const editor = window.Waymark_Map_Factory.editor();

children.value: object, are used to customise the Editor Mode.
Whether to show a confirmation message when deleting an object.
The ID of a element to output the GeoJSON into. By default this is a
This configuration disables the confirmation message when deleting an object and specifies a custom container for the GeoJSON output.
Once the configuration is set, the
method is called to initialise the Map:
The Map is now displayed on the page according to the provided options.

method, which accepts a GeoJSON object as an argument.

promise to ensure the data is available:

contents.title: Customise
contents.description: Watmark JS was designed to be customisable and flexible.

children.value: Watmark JS was designed to be customisable and flexible.
In addition to the available options, you can customise Waymark JS using CSS and JavaScript.
The Map above has lots of (Patriotic) customisations to help illustrate some of the concepts outlined here. Be sure to view the
Waymark has lots of options so you can create custom Maps with Overlays to suit your needs.
Be sure to check out the full list of:
– for setting up the Map view, including custom
– enabling and configuring Viewer features, including the Image Gallery, Overlay Filter, Marker Clustering and Elevation Profile.
– to customise the Editor Mode, including the GeoJSON output and confirmation messages.
All text displayed by Waymark JS is fully customisable and can be translated/localised into any language.
You can modify the default strings using the
option when initialising the Map. In the example above, all text displayed by the Editor user interface will be in French.
The full list of localisation strings is:

props.code:
const english = {
  // Common (Viewer & Editor)
  action_fullscreen_activate: "View Fullscreen",
  action_fullscreen_deactivate: "Exit Fullscreen",
  action_locate_activate: "Show me where I am",
  action_zoom_in: "Zoom in",
  action_zoom_out: "Zoom out",
  label_total_length: "Total Length: ",
  label_max_elevation: "Max. Elevation: ",
  label_min_elevation: "Min. Elevation: ",
  label_ascent: "Total Ascent: ",
  label_descent: "Total Descent: ",

  // Editor only
  add_line_title: "Draw a Line",
  add_photo_title: "Upload an Image",
  add_marker_title: "Place a Marker",
  add_rectangle_title: "Draw a Rectangle",
  add_polygon_title: "Draw a Polygon",
  add_circle_title: "Draw a Circle",
  upload_file_title:
    "Read Lines and Markers from file (GPX/KML/GeoJSON supported, which most apps should Export to)",
  action_duplicate: "Duplicate",
  action_delete: "Delete",
  action_edit: "Edit",
  action_edit_done: "Finish editing",
  action_upload_image: "Upload Image",
  object_title_placeholder: "Title",
  object_image_placeholder: "Image URL",
  object_description_placeholder: "Description",
  object_type_label: "Type",
  marker_latlng_label: "Lat,Lng",
  action_delete_confirm: "Are you sure you want to delete this",
  action_search_placeholder: "Search...",
  object_label_marker: "Marker",
  object_label_line: "Line",
  object_label_shape: "Shape",
  object_label_marker_plural: "Markers",
  object_label_line_plural: "Lines",
  object_label_shape_plural: "Shapes",
  error_message_prefix: "Waymark Error",
  info_message_prefix: "Waymark Info",
  debug_message_prefix: "Waymark Debug",
  error_file_type: "This file type is not supported.",
  error_file_conversion: "Could not convert this file to GeoJSON.",
  error_file_upload: "File upload error.",
  error_photo_meta: "Could not retrieve Image metadata.",
  info_exif_yes: "Image location metadata (EXIF) detected!",
  info_exif_no: "Image location metadata (EXIF) NOT detected.",
  error_no_wpmedia: "WordPress Media Library not found",
  no_direction: "No Direction",
  show_direction: "Show Direction",
  reverse_direction: "Reverse Direction",
  sleep_wake_message: "Click or Hover to Wake",
};

children.value: "Read Lines and Markers from file (GPX/KML/GeoJSON supported, which most apps should Export to)"
"Are you sure you want to delete this"

If a translation is not provided, the default English string will be used.
